source_issue.title: Check reserved words on creating new content type instances

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a bug where editors can create content using reserved words (like "register"), which conflict with existing functionality (in this case, a registration form). The goal is to prevent the creation of such content by managing the IDs more effectively.

## What needs to be done
1. Identify the reserved words from the Volto routes (see [`src/routes.js`](https://github.com/plone/volto/blob/master/src/routes.js)).
2. When creating content with a reserved name, append "-1" to the ID to ensure uniqueness, similar to the backend process.
3. Implement an acceptance test to check for at least one reserved word, such as "register".

## Helpful background
The issue revolves around preventing collisions with reserved keywords that are used for routing in the frontend. Understanding how IDs are generated and handled in Plone's architecture is essential.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the specified routes and how they define reserved words.
2. Review existing code in [`Add.jsx`](https://github.com/plone/volto/blob/master/src/components/manage/Add/Add.jsx#L193-L212) for content creation.
3. Consider how to run tests against the reserved words, referencing settings in `config.settings`.

If you're new to the codebase, don't hesitate to ask questions or seek clarification from the community!
```
